title: rubus villicaulis subsp. confinis
intro: Rubus villicaulis subsp. confinis, also known as the New Zealand blackthorn or southern thimbleberry, is a subspecies of the rubus villicaulis plant, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been found to exhibit medicinal properties in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus villicaulis subsp. confinis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and possess antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
